model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"],"rationale":"Revert the underperforming AdamW/cosine change and spend the budget on a slightly deeper, wider CNN, which is the most likely way to beat the current shallow-model baseline on CIFAR-10.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The deeper two-conv-block model is likely under-stepped at the current Adam rate; a modest LR increase should improve optimization within the fixed 256-step budget without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"Undo the overshot learning-rate increase and return to the value that already produced the best visible validation loss, since the deeper CNN appears sensitive to step size under the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Add mild label smoothing to reduce overconfidence and improve validation loss without disturbing the now-stable optimization setup.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"The current recipe is now stable, so the next likely gain is a modest width increase in both the feature stack and classifier to reduce underfitting while keeping the successful optimizer and label-smoothing setup unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Undo the width increase that regressed validation loss and return to the previously best-performing compact configuration while keeping the successful label smoothing in place.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"Activate cosine decay over the fixed step budget so the model can move quickly early and anneal smoothly toward the end, which is a low-complexity way to squeeze more out of the current undertrained regime.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"],"rationale":"The cosine schedule regressed validation loss, so the next clean test is a decoupled-weight-decay optimizer with a slightly smaller Adam-style learning rate, keeping the successful architecture and label smoothing intact.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"],"rationale":"Restore the Adam configuration that proved stable, then raise weight decay slightly from the under-regularized baseline to see if the current architecture can generalize a bit better without changing the rest of the working recipe.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Restore the lighter weight decay that matched the best visible validation loss; the stronger regularization path did not improve on the compact CNN under this budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"A slightly stronger label-smoothing regularizer is the smallest remaining change likely to improve calibration and validation loss now that the optimizer and weight decay are back on the stable branch.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The stronger smoothing regressed validation loss, so restore the weaker 0.05 setting that previously gave the best visible result.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"With only 256 optimizer steps, a larger batch lets each step consume more of CIFAR-10 and can improve stability without changing the successful model or optimizer setup.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The current recipe improved sharply when batch size doubled; with only 256 optimizer steps, another increase should expose more of CIFAR-10 per update and may further reduce validation loss without changing the rest of the stable setup.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The previous batch-size doublings produced large gains, so with 256 fixed steps the next likely win is to expose the full dataset more aggressively by doubling batch size again.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The prior doubling to 512 still improved validation loss, so another increase should further raise data throughput per step and may squeeze out more performance within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"Batch-size scaling has been the dominant source of gains, and a further moderate increase should expose more of CIFAR-10 per step while keeping the rest of the stable recipe unchanged.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","anchor":"","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":[],"rationale":"The larger batch regressed validation loss, so restore the previously best-performing 1024-batch setting as the safest final move.","secondary_modes":[]}
